skill.name: biopython-alignment
skill.description: Sequence alignment and alignment file processing with Biopython (Bio.Align/Bio.AlignIO), triggered when you need global/local pairwise alignment, MSA read/write/format conversion, or alignment statistics/filtering.
skill.summary: Performs pairwise and multiple sequence alignment operations using Biopython, including format conversion and conservation analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All high-severity findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match BLOSUM substitution matrices (standard bioinformatics scoring, not encryption). All 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code fences in documentation files, not actual shell execution. The only confirmed finding is a benign file write in msa_conservation.py:74 that writes conservation results to a config-controlled output path. No malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration detected.

medium_findings.title: File write to configurable output path
medium_findings.description: Script writes MSA conservation analysis results to a file path read from task_config.json. The file path is config-controlled and the write operation is the intended output behavior of the script. Low risk as path is not derived from untrusted user input.

content.user_title: Perform DNA and Protein Sequence Alignment with Biopython
content.value_statement: Bioinformatics researchers need to align genetic and protein sequences but writing alignment code from scratch is complex and error-prone. This skill uses Biopython to handle pairwise alignment, multiple sequence alignment operations, format conversion, and column-wise conservation analysis through ready-to-use scripts and guided instructions.
actual_capabilities: Performs global and local pairwise sequence alignment using Bio.Align.PairwiseAligner
Reads, writes, and converts multiple sequence alignment files between FASTA, Clustal, Stockholm, and PHYLIP formats
Computes column-wise conservation scores for multiple sequence alignments using a configurable threshold
Configures substitution matrices (BLOSUM, PAM) and gap penalty parameters for biologically meaningful scoring
Extracts the best matching subsequence fragment from local pairwise alignments
limitations: Does not perform de novo multiple sequence alignment; requires pre-aligned input sequences
Does not support phylogenetic tree reconstruction or evolutionary rate analysis
Does not process raw sequencing reads; requires assembled sequences as input
Alignment quality depends on user-provided scoring parameters and substitution matrices

use_cases.title: Compare Two Protein Sequences for Homology
use_cases.description: A molecular biologist wants to determine if two uncharacterized protein sequences share structural similarity. Use global pairwise alignment with the BLOSUM62 matrix to compute an alignment score and visualize the aligned residues.

use_cases.title: Find Conserved Regions Across Species
use_cases.description: A comparative genomics researcher needs to identify conserved nucleotide positions across a multiple sequence alignment of orthologous genes. Use the MSA conservation script to quantify column-wise conservation and output positions above a threshold.

use_cases.title: Convert Alignment Files Between Bioinformatics Formats
use_cases.description: A data analyst receives a Clustal alignment file but needs PHYLIP format for downstream phylogenetic software. Use Bio.AlignIO to convert between supported alignment formats while preserving sequence metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pairwise DNA Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Align two short DNA sequences using Biopython PairwiseAligner with default parameters and show the alignment result.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user learning sequence alignment basics

prompt_templates.title: Global Protein Alignment with BLOSUM62
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform global protein alignment using Biopython PairwiseAligner with the BLOSUM62 substitution matrix, open gap penalty of -10, and extension penalty of -0.5.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher comparing two protein sequences for homology detection

prompt_templates.title: MSA Conservation Analysis from Config
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the task configuration from config/task_config.json, run the msa_conservation.py script to compute conservation scores, and output positions with conservation above 0.8.
prompt_templates.scenario: Computational biologist screening an alignment for highly conserved functional sites

prompt_templates.title: Alignment Format Conversion and Identity Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Read a Stockholm format alignment file, convert it to PHYLIP format using AlignIO.convert, then compute all pairwise sequence identity percentages from the alignment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Bioinformatician preparing data for downstream phylogenetic analysis tools

output_examples.input: Global protein alignment with BLOSUM62 for two hemoglobin alpha sequences
output_examples.output: A text report showing the global alignment of two protein sequences with the BLOSUM62 score, aligned strings with gap positions, and match/mismatch annotations between the sequences.

output_examples.input: Column conservation analysis for a 4-sequence MSA of length 8
output_examples.output: A column-wise conservation table listing each alignment column index, the consensus residue, conservation fraction, and number of non-gap sequences contributing to the calculation.

output_examples.input: Convert a Clustal alignment to PHYLIP format
output_examples.output: A confirmation message showing the number of sequences, alignment length, output file path, and format conversion result for the alignment file.

best_practices: Use the BLOSUM62 substitution matrix for protein alignments and match/mismatch scores for nucleotide alignments to obtain biologically meaningful results
Configure gap opening penalties higher than extension penalties (e.g., -10 open, -0.5 extend) for realistic alignment gap distribution
Store runtime configuration parameters in the config JSON file to maintain reproducibility and avoid long CLI argument lists
anti_patterns: Using global alignment mode for sequences of very different lengths; local mode finds the best matching fragment more effectively
Applying nucleotide match and mismatch scores to protein sequences without a substitution matrix, which ignores biochemical similarity
Ignoring gap characters when computing conservation statistics across MSA columns, leading to inflated conservation scores in gapped regions

faq.question: What is the difference between global and local alignment?
faq.answer: Global alignment aligns two sequences end-to-end using Needleman-Wunsch algorithm. Local alignment finds the best matching fragment between sequences using Smith-Waterman algorithm.

faq.question: Which substitution matrix should I use for protein alignment?
faq.answer: BLOSUM62 is the default recommendation for most protein alignments. Use BLOSUM80 for closely related sequences and BLOSUM45 for distant evolutionary relationships.

faq.question: How do I convert an alignment from FASTA to Clustal format?
faq.answer: Use Bio.AlignIO.convert() with the input format set to fasta and output format set to clustal. The function returns the number of sequences converted.

faq.question: What gap penalty values should I use?
faq.answer: Common defaults are open gap penalty of -10 and extension penalty of -0.5 for protein alignments. For nucleotides, use -2 for open gap and -0.5 for extension.

faq.question: Can this skill create a multiple sequence alignment from unaligned sequences?
faq.answer: No, this skill works with pre-aligned sequences only. Use external tools like Clustal Omega or MUSCLE for de novo multiple sequence alignment.

faq.question: How is column conservation calculated in the MSA script?
faq.answer: Conservation per column is the count of the most common non-gap residue divided by the total non-gap count in that column. Values range from 0 to 1.
